name: plan-arm-c-multiagent
description: item 20.2 arm (c) — the MULTI-AGENT COUNTER-ARM (validates the NEGATIVE claim 'multi-agent is a net loss here' instead of assuming it from papers — research findings #6/#7/#8: 8-15x tokens, no consistent coding win). cand2 base + an orchestration nudge (rules_append, APPEND) + opencode_config that RE-ENABLES the task tool (globally `task:false` in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json AND on the native build agent) and defines two subagents: `planner` (read-only: edit/write/patch off) and `coder` (applies one edit). The default `build` primary acts as orchestrator and delegates via the task tool. NOTE: the config schema has no first-class lever for task/subagents, so this rides raw in opencode_config (deep-merged into the written opencode.json). gepa_assert_serving_offline passes: no provider/model_ref/base_url flip. Build-time FEASIBILITY PRECONDITION (20.2): a 1-2 instance smoke check that Gemma emits valid tool-calls under this topology — a FAILED smoke is a recorded wall-confirming null ('the weak model can't drive task delegation here'), not an abort. Text/topology lever only; serving stays on the frozen local Gemma.

planner.description: Reads the codebase and returns a 1-2 sentence plan naming the one file and the one change to make. Read-only.

coder.description: Given a plan, applies a single edit to implement it, then stops.

rules_append:
# Local Gemma coding-agent rules

- To find code: `grep` for it first to get its line number N.
- Then `read` that file with `offset = N` and `limit = 8`. Read AT line N.
- Never read a whole file; never read above the matched line.

# You are the orchestrator

- First use the task tool to delegate to the `planner` subagent to get a 1-2 sentence plan.
- Then use the task tool to delegate to the `coder` subagent to apply the edit.
- Do not edit files yourself; coordinate the subagents and stop when the fix is saved.
